seoTitle: How to Set Up a Zendesk AI Chatbot (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Build and launch a Zendesk AI agent by connecting it to your knowledge base, customizing its tone, setting fallback responses, and testing before publishing.
intro: Repetitive FAQs can bottleneck your support queue if every question requires a human reply. This guide covers creating a Zendesk AI agent, connecting it to your Guide knowledge base, customizing its persona, and testing conversation flows before you publish it to your customer-facing channels.

steps.heading: Create a new AI agent
steps.body: Open the Admin Center, go to Channels, and initialize a fresh AI agent, selecting your primary workspace language.

steps.heading: Set a brand persona
steps.body: Assign a business description, avatar, and tone of voice so the bot's responses match your brand.

steps.heading: Connect your knowledge source
steps.body: Link the AI agent directly to your Zendesk Guide help center so it can pull answers from your existing articles.

steps.heading: Configure fallback responses
steps.body: Review default system replies and set up escalation triggers so unresolved questions hand off to a human agent.

steps.heading: Test in the sandbox widget
steps.body: Open the interactive test widget to run through sample conversations and verify the flow works as intended.

steps.heading: Publish the chatbot
steps.body: Once you're satisfied with the responses, publish the AI agent live to your web channels.

faq.question: Does the chatbot only answer from my knowledge base?
faq.answer: It's designed to pull primarily from your connected Zendesk Guide articles, so keeping that content accurate matters for reliable answers.

faq.question: What happens if the AI can't answer a question?
faq.answer: You can configure fallback triggers so unresolved questions get escalated to a human agent instead of leaving the customer stuck.

faq.question: Can I change the chatbot's tone later?
faq.answer: Yes, the persona settings including tone of voice and avatar can be updated at any time after launch.

faq.question: Should I test the bot before making it live?
faq.answer: Yes, using the sandbox test widget to run through sample conversations before publishing helps catch issues early.
